chapter_question: How does exterior differentiation turn local variation into a form?
inspection_targets: The operation sends p-forms to p+1-forms and squares to zero.
The derivative of a 1-form is seen around a tiny cell.
Degree controls the sign in the product rule.
Topology separates closed from globally exact behavior.
Gradient, curl, and divergence fit one exterior derivative chain.

source_span.focus: Introduces the exterior derivative and shows how it unifies differential operations in vector calculus and physics.
source_span.label: Chapter 36
source_span.title: Differentiation
topics: The fundamental identity d squared equals zero
Gradient, curl, and divergence in form language
